item.title: Loyal Equipments Ltd Falls 2.46%: Technical Upgrade and Valuation Shift Shape Volatile Week
item.description: Loyal Equipments Ltd experienced a turbulent week on the BSE, closing at Rs.180.55 on 6 March 2026, down 2.46% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.185.10. Despite this decline, the stock outperformed the broader Sensex, which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by a significant upgrade in the company’s technical and valuation outlook, a sharp intraday rally, and notable volatility in price and volume, reflecting a complex interplay of cautious optimism and persistent financial challenges. Time: Mar 15 2026 05:01 PM IST

item.title: Ircon International Ltd Gains 2.37%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: Ircon International Ltd’s stock showed a mixed week from 2 to 6 March 2026, closing with a modest gain of 2.37% to Rs.146.95, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by sharp intraday volatility, new 52-week lows early in the week, followed by a strong rebound driven by heavy institutional buying and volume surges on the final trading day. Time: Mar 15 2026 05:01 PM IST

item.title: W S Industries Falls 1.40%: 2 Key Technical Signals Driving the Decline
item.description: W S Industries (India) Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing at Rs.69.70 on 6 March 2026, down 1.40% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.70.69. This decline came amid a broader market sell-off, with the Sensex falling 3.00% over the same period. Despite the stock’s relative resilience compared to the benchmark, technical developments including the formation of a Death Cross and a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating intensified bearish sentiment, signalling potential further weakness ahead. Time: Mar 15 2026 05:01 PM IST

item.title: eClerx Services Ltd Declines 4.80%: Technical Shifts and Market Volatility Shape the Week
item.description: eClerx Services Ltd experienced a turbulent week marked by significant technical momentum shifts and a 4.80% decline in its stock price, closing at Rs.3,021.35 on 6 March 2026. Despite outperforming the Sensex's 3.00% fall over the same period, the stock faced mixed signals from technical indicators and a downgrade to a Hold rating, reflecting cautious investor sentiment amid broader market volatility. Time: Mar 15 2026 05:01 PM IST

item.title: Seasons Textiles Gains 9.44%: Key Valuation Shift Amidst Weak Fundamentals
item.description: Seasons Textiles Ltd closed the week with a notable 9.44% gain, rising from Rs.18.00 to Rs.19.70, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. Despite the strong price appreciation on the final trading day, the week was marked by a downgrade to a ‘Strong Sell’ rating and a nuanced shift in valuation from very attractive to attractive, reflecting mixed financial signals and subdued fundamentals. Time: Mar 15 2026 05:01 PM IST

item.title: Mangalore Refinery & Petrochemicals Ltd Gains 4.93%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Rally
item.description: Mangalore Refinery & Petrochemicals Ltd. (MRPL) closed the week ending 6 March 2026 with a notable gain of 4.93%, rising from Rs.196.65 to Rs.206.35. This performance stands in stark contrast to the Sensex, which declined 3.00% over the same period, underscoring MRPL’s relative strength amid a volatile market backdrop. The week was marked by a sharp opening gap down, followed by a strong recovery and a surge to new 52-week highs, supported by robust volume and an upgraded Mojo Grade. Time: Mar 15 2026 05:01 PM IST

item.title: JTL Industries Drops 16.12%: 6 Key Factors Behind the Steep Decline
item.description: JTL Industries Ltd endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price plummeting 16.12% from Rs.63.70 to Rs.53.43, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% decline over the same period. The week was marked by steep gap downs, a fresh 52-week low, and persistent selling pressure amid subdued financial results and cautious market sentiment. Time: Mar 15 2026 05:00 PM IST
